topic: A student learns their scholarship application was denied
raw_output:
[story 1]

The email sat glowing on the screen, stark white against the dark backdrop of my laptop. My thumb hovered over the 'reply' button, a tremor running through my hand before I finally clicked. The words swam before my eyes—a polite, impersonal rejection. A heavy, cold weight settled in my chest, squeezing the air from my lungs. I reread the rejection letter three times, each sentence landing with the dull thud of a stone in my stomach. I leaned back in my chair, the cheap plastic groaning in protest, and stared blankly at the ceiling, tracing the cracks in the paint with my gaze. The silence of the room amplified the frantic drumming of my own pulse. I felt a sudden, overwhelming urge to curl into a tight ball, to disappear into the worn fabric of my hoodie, wishing the floor would just swallow me whole.

[story 2]

I walked across the quad, the late afternoon sun doing nothing to warm the chill that had taken root inside me. Every passerby seemed to move with an effortless buoyancy, their conversations light and carefree. I kept my head down, acutely aware of the weight of my backpack, which suddenly felt like it was filled with lead. When I finally reached the library steps, I didn't bother with the main entrance; I slipped into a secluded alcove, the shadows offering a temporary shield. Pulling out the rejection notification, I didn't cry. Instead, my jaw tightened until my teeth ached, and I gripped the paper so hard my knuckles turned white. A slow, shaky exhale escaped me, a sound ragged and thin. I felt a profound sense of deflation, as if the entire structure of my hopes had just crumbled into dust beneath my feet.

[story 3]

The lukewarm coffee in my hand tasted like ash. I stared at the application portal, the screen mocking me with its final, unforgiving verdict. A slow, deliberate movement brought my hand up to my mouth, pressing my lips together until they were painfully thin. The disappointment wasn't a sudden shock; it was a creeping tide, rising steadily until it submerged everything else. I let my shoulders slump, the tension draining out of me until I felt strangely hollowed out. I began to pace the small space between my desk and the window, my steps uneven, a restless energy trapped beneath a surface of absolute stillness. Each footfall echoed the emptiness in my ambition, a hollow sound in the quiet room.
